 @import "tailwindcss";
 
 /* ============================================================================
-   ActivationOps — "Ledger Enterprise" design system (Stage 1 foundation)
-   Ported from mockups/v3-ledger-enterprise.html. Premium white ground, a single
-   vermilion accent (two lightnesses of one hue), cool-neutral ink/graphite, and
-   one signature primitive: the hairline-ruled ledger row (field · value · verdict).
-   Fonts are self-hosted via next/font/google (see app/layout.tsx) and reach this
-   file as CSS variables (--font-sans / --font-mono / --font-geist-mono).
+   Curbside Commons — "Oxblood" site design system (S1 foundation, 2026-07-08)
+   Spec: mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/whole-site-gallery-v2.html (owner-picked).
+
+   RETHEME-IN-PLACE: every :root token NAME below is preserved (layout.tsx, Nav,
+   and the landing consume them) but the VALUES are remapped to Oxblood — a premium
+   off-white ground (#FBFBFD), a warm near-black ink ramp (harmonizing with the
+   accent, not cool-gray), wine-tinted hairlines, and ONE burgundy accent (#8A2233,
+   two lightnesses of one hue). An ADDITIVE "OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1)" section holds
+   the site-scoped tokens (wine ramp / severity / serif / ease) and the shared shell
+   primitives (nav + footer). Fonts still self-host via next/font/google (see
+   app/layout.tsx) as --font-sans / --font-mono / --font-geist-mono.
+
+   INTENTIONALLY UNTOUCHED (byte-for-byte, superseded by later slices):
+   • the entire `.rpt-wrap` report/demo Ledger block (line ~1105 → its print block)
+     — it redefines --paper/--ink/--body/--graphite/--hairline/--um/--sev-* locally,
+     so it is insulated from these :root remaps (S3 re-skins it to Oxblood);
+   • all `.ds-*` / `.cp-*` / `.lp-*` landing rule blocks — they inherit the new token
+     VALUES (new colors, old layout = the expected hybrid until S2 supersedes them).
    ========================================================================== */
 
 :root {
   color-scheme: light;
 
-  /* ground — true premium white, no warmth */
-  --bg: #ffffff;
-  --bg-2: #fcfcfd;
-  --bg-3: #f7f7f9;
-
-  /* ink + graphite structure (cool neutrals, AA+ on white) */
-  --ink: #0a0a0c;
-  --ink-2: #16161a;
-  --body: #34343c; /* 11.6:1 — body text */
-  --graphite: #52525c; /* 7.72:1 — labels, values */
-  --muted: #5d5d68; /* 6.50:1 — secondary mono */
-  --faint: #6c6c78; /* 5.16:1 — non-essential ≥13px */
-
-  /* hairlines */
-  --rule: #ececef;
-  --rule-2: #e0e0e4;
-  --rule-3: #d2d2d8;
-
-  /* THE single accent — vermilion, two lightnesses of one hue */
-  --signal: #e2402a; /* fills, dots, rules, marks */
-  --signal-text: #c0301b; /* 5.71:1 on white — small labels / verdicts */
-  --signal-tint: #fbedea; /* faint held-row ground */
-  --signal-rule: #f0d6cf; /* hairline at signal hue */
+  /* ground — premium off-white (NOT pure #fff; pure white+black is an AI-median
+     tell). Warm-neutral steps down. */
+  --bg: #fbfbfd;
+  --bg-2: #f8f7f8;
+  --bg-3: #f5f3f4;
+
+  /* ink + graphite structure — a WARM near-black ramp (R>G>B), harmonizing with
+     the wine accent. Contrast recomputed on the #fbfbfd ground; every step clears
+     the prior cool-gray token's documented ratio. */
+  --ink: #1a1712; /* 17.29:1 — headings, hard marks */
+  --ink-2: #2e2825; /* 14.05:1 — strong text */
+  --body: #423b38; /* 10.61:1 — body text (spec #463f3c was 9.98:1, darkened to clear ≥10:1) */
+  --graphite: #564d48; /* 7.97:1 — labels, values (≥ old 7.72) */
+  --muted: #615750; /* 6.80:1 — secondary mono (≥ old 6.50) */
+  --faint: #6d635b; /* 5.67:1 — non-essential ≥13px (≥ old 5.16) */
+
+  /* hairlines — wine-tinted (rgba of the wine-900 hue), darkening steps */
+  --rule: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.1);
+  --rule-2: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.17);
+  --rule-3: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.26);
+
+  /* THE single accent — burgundy / oxblood, two lightnesses of one hue */
+  --signal: #8a2233; /* fills, dots, rules, marks */
+  --signal-text: #8a2233; /* 8.62:1 on #fbfbfd — small labels / verdicts (≥4.5) */
+  --signal-tint: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.07); /* faint held-row ground */
+  --signal-rule: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.24); /* hairline at signal hue */
 
   /* spacing — 4px base */
   --s1: 4px;
   --s2: 8px;
   --s3: 12px;
   --s4: 16px;
   --s5: 24px;
   --s6: 32px;
   --s7: 48px;
   --s8: 64px;
   --s9: 88px;
   --s10: 120px;
   --maxw: 1180px;
 
   /* type — font stacks resolve to the next/font CSS vars, with safe fallbacks */
   --ff-sans: var(--font-sans), system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
   --ff-mono: var(--font-mono), var(--font-geist-mono), ui-monospace, monospace;
 
+  /* ==== OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1, 2026-07-08) — additive site-scoped tokens ==== */
+  /* wine ramp — the ONE brand hue, wine-900 (darkest) → wine-500 */
+  --wine-900: #3e0a13;
+  --wine-800: #55101d;
+  --wine-700: #6e1423;
+  --wine-600: #8a2233;
+  --wine-500: #9e3143;
+  /* accent aliases (the site layer reads these; = the wine ramp) */
+  --accent: #8a2233;
+  --accent-hi: #6e1423; /* hover / pressed */
+  --accent-soft: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.07);
+  --accent-line: rgba(138, 34, 51, 0.24);
+  /* severity — kept perceptually FAR from the wine brand (always paired with a
+     shape + word by consuming surfaces, never colour alone) */
+  --error: #d92d20;
+  --error-soft: rgba(217, 45, 32, 0.08);
+  --warn: #b54708;
+  --warn-soft: rgba(181, 71, 8, 0.11);
+  --ok: #067647;
+  --ok-soft: rgba(6, 118, 71, 0.09);
+  /* serif display stack (brand wordmark + headlines) — no new dependency */
+  --serif: ui-serif, "New York", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Palatino, Georgia,
+    "Times New Roman", serif;
+  /* shared easing */
+  --ease: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
+  /* ==== end OXBLOOD SITE LAYER tokens ==== */
+
   font-synthesis: none;
   text-rendering: optimizeLegibility;
   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
   -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
 }
 
 * {
   box-sizing: border-box;
 }
 
 html {
   scroll-behavior: smooth;
 }
 
 body {
   margin: 0;
   background-color: var(--bg);
   color: var(--body);
   font-family: var(--ff-sans);
   font-size: 17px;
   line-height: 1.6;
   letter-spacing: -0.005em;
   -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
 }
 
+/* ============================================================================
+   OXBLOOD SITE LAYER (S1, 2026-07-08) — shared shell primitives (Nav + footer).
+   Only what the global shell needs; landing/report/demo surfaces keep their own
+   blocks. Consumed by components/Nav.tsx and app/layout.tsx. Element selectors
+   `nav` / `footer` are hidden in the report @media print block (unchanged).
+   ========================================================================== */
+
+/* ----- Nav: sticky, hairline-ruled, serif wordmark, sans links (mockup spec) - */
+.site-nav {
+  position: sticky;
+  top: 0;
+  z-index: 40;
+  /* rgba(255,255,255,.88) IS the no-support fallback; blur is progressive */
+  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.88);
+  backdrop-filter: saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
+  -webkit-backdrop-filter: saturate(1.4) blur(10px);
+  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
+}
+.site-nav-in {
+  max-width: var(--maxw);
+  margin: 0 auto;
+  display: flex;
+  flex-wrap: wrap;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 4px;
+  padding: 11px clamp(18px, 4vw, 36px);
+}
+.site-brand {
+  display: inline-flex;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 9px;
+  margin-right: 18px;
+  font-family: var(--serif);
+  font-weight: 600;
+  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
+  font-size: 16px;
+  color: var(--ink);
+  text-decoration: none;
+}
+.site-brand-mark {
+  width: 22px;
+  height: 22px;
+  flex: none;
+  color: var(--accent);
+}
+.site-navlink {
+  /* mockup spec: sans 13px (E-3 reviewer fix — the dispatch brief's "mono" was
+     the orchestrator's error; the mockup .navlink inherits the sans body font) */
+  font-family: var(--ff-sans);
+  font-size: 13px;
+  color: var(--muted);
+  text-decoration: none;
+  padding: 6px 11px;
+  border-radius: 7px;
+  white-space: nowrap;
+  transition:
+    background 0.16s var(--ease),
+    color 0.16s ease;
+}
+.site-navlink:hover {
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  color: var(--ink);
+}
+.site-navlink[aria-current="page"] {
+  background: var(--accent);
+  color: #fff;
+}
+.site-navlink:focus-visible,
+.site-brand:focus-visible {
+  outline: 2px solid var(--accent);
+  outline-offset: 2px;
+}
+.site-status {
+  margin-left: auto;
+  display: inline-flex;
+  align-items: center;
+  gap: 7px;
+  font-family: var(--ff-mono);
+  font-size: 11px;
+  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
+  color: var(--muted);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  border-radius: 999px;
+  padding: 5px 11px;
+}
+.site-status-pip {
+  width: 6px;
+  height: 6px;
+  border-radius: 50%;
+  background: var(--accent);
+  flex: none;
+}
+
+/* ----- global footer: honesty paragraph on a hairline-ruled panel ----------- */
+.site-footer {
+  margin-top: 20px;
+  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  background: var(--bg-3);
+}
+.site-footer-in {
+  max-width: var(--maxw);
+  margin: 0 auto;
+  padding: 40px clamp(18px, 4vw, 36px) 72px;
+  font-size: 13px;
+  line-height: 1.62;
+  color: var(--body);
+}
+
+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
+  .site-navlink {
+    transition: none;
+  }
+}
+
 /* ----- type primitives ------------------------------------------------------ */
 
 .ds-mono {
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
   font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1;
 }
 .ds-num {
   font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
   font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1;
 }
 
 .ds-h1,
 .ds-h2,
 .ds-h3,
 .ds-h4 {
   color: var(--ink);
   margin: 0;
   font-weight: 700;
   line-height: 1.04;
   letter-spacing: -0.025em;
   text-wrap: balance;
 }
 .ds-h2 {
   letter-spacing: -0.028em;
 }
 .ds-h3 {
   font-weight: 600;
   letter-spacing: -0.018em;
 }
 .ds-h4 {
   font-weight: 600;
   letter-spacing: -0.012em;
 }
 
 .ds-h2big {
   font-size: clamp(28px, 4vw, 42px);
   margin-top: 18px;
 }
 
 .ds-lede {
   font-size: 20px;
   line-height: 1.5;
   color: var(--body);
   max-width: 62ch;
   letter-spacing: -0.01em;
 }
 .ds-lede em {
   font-style: normal;
   color: var(--ink);
   font-weight: 600;
 }
 
 /* sentence-case mono eyebrow — NOT all-caps (anti-slop) */
 .ds-eyebrow {
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-size: 12.5px;
   font-weight: 500;
   letter-spacing: 0.01em;
   color: var(--graphite);
   display: inline-flex;
   align-items: baseline;
   gap: 10px;
 }
 .ds-eyebrow .idx {
   color: var(--signal-text);
   font-weight: 600;
 }
 
 /* ----- layout rhythm -------------------------------------------------------- */
 
 .ds-wrap {
   max-width: var(--maxw);
   margin: 0 auto;
   padding: 0 32px;
 }
 @media (max-width: 560px) {
   .ds-wrap {
     padding: 0 20px;
   }
@@ -355,176 +512,181 @@ body {
 }
 
 /* Scroll-reveal helpers — resolved to settled state under reduced motion. */
 .ds-reveal {
   opacity: 0;
   transform: translateY(12px);
   transition:
     opacity 0.6s ease,
     transform 0.6s ease;
 }
 .ds-reveal.in {
   opacity: 1;
   transform: none;
 }
 .ds-stagger > * {
   opacity: 0;
   transform: translateY(8px);
   transition:
     opacity 0.5s ease,
     transform 0.5s ease;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > * {
   opacity: 1;
   transform: none;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > *:nth-child(1) {
   transition-delay: 0.04s;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > *:nth-child(2) {
   transition-delay: 0.12s;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > *:nth-child(3) {
   transition-delay: 0.2s;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > *:nth-child(4) {
   transition-delay: 0.28s;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > *:nth-child(5) {
   transition-delay: 0.36s;
 }
 .ds-stagger.in > *:nth-child(6) {
   transition-delay: 0.44s;
 }
 
 /* ----- the CatchPanel centerpiece (cp-*) ------------------------------------ */
 /* SSR renders settled (.cp-settled or .cp-on). Animation only via .cp class
    without .cp-on (un-resolved), resolved by .cp-on or .cp-settled. */
 .cp-cap {
   display: flex;
   align-items: center;
   justify-content: space-between;
   gap: 12px;
   padding: 13px 16px;
   border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule-2);
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-size: 11.5px;
   letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   color: var(--graphite);
   background: var(--bg-2);
 }
 .cp-cap-right {
   color: var(--signal-text);
   display: inline-flex;
   align-items: center;
   gap: 7px;
 }
 .cp-tick {
   width: 6px;
   height: 6px;
   border-radius: 999px;
   background: var(--signal);
   flex: none;
 }
 
 .cp-record {
   padding: 13px 16px;
   background: var(--bg-2);
   border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);
   display: flex;
   flex-wrap: wrap;
-  gap: 6px 18px;
+  gap: 8px;
   align-items: baseline;
 }
 .cp-rlbl {
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-size: 11px;
   color: var(--graphite);
   letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   width: 100%;
-  margin-bottom: 2px;
+  margin-bottom: 4px;
 }
+/* record fields as hairline chips (Oxblood v2 catch — matches the mockup .rf .f) */
 .cp-rf {
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-size: 11.5px;
   color: var(--ink);
   white-space: nowrap;
+  background: var(--bg);
+  border: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  border-radius: 6px;
+  padding: 3px 8px;
 }
 .cp-rf .cp-rk {
   color: var(--graphite);
 }
 .cp-rf.absent .cp-rv {
   color: var(--signal-text);
 }
 .cp-rf .cp-rv {
   font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
 }
 
 .cp-draft {
   padding: 0;
 }
 .cp-claim {
   padding: 16px 16px;
   border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
   opacity: 1;
   transform: none;
   transition:
     opacity 0.5s ease,
     transform 0.5s ease,
     background 0.45s ease;
 }
 .cp-claim:first-child {
   border-top: none;
 }
 .cp-claim.fail {
   background: var(--signal-tint);
 }
 .cp-ctext {
   font-size: 15.5px;
   line-height: 1.45;
   color: var(--ink);
   letter-spacing: -0.01em;
 }
 .cp-verdict {
   margin-top: 11px;
   display: flex;
   align-items: center;
   gap: 12px;
   flex-wrap: wrap;
   font-family: var(--ff-mono);
   font-size: 11.5px;
   letter-spacing: 0.005em;
   opacity: 1;
   transition: opacity 0.35s ease;
 }
 .cp-vcell {
   display: inline-flex;
   align-items: center;
   gap: 8px;
   font-weight: 500;
   padding: 3px 0;
 }
 .cp-vcell.pass {
   color: var(--ink);
 }
 .cp-vcell.fail {
   color: var(--signal-text);
 }
 .cp-glyph {
   width: 15px;
   height: 15px;
   flex: none;
   transform: scale(1);
   opacity: 1;
   transition:
     transform 0.42s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1),
     opacity 0.3s ease;
 }
 .cp-cite {
   color: var(--muted);
 }
 
 .cp-held {
   padding: 16px 16px;
   border-top: 1px solid var(--signal-rule);
   background: var(--signal-tint);
   display: flex;
@@ -556,715 +718,615 @@ body {
   align-items: center;
   gap: 10px;
   flex-wrap: wrap;
   opacity: 1;
   transition: opacity 0.5s ease;
 }
 .cp-dnote {
   font-size: 11px;
   color: var(--muted);
   margin-right: auto;
 }
 
 /* Animated (motion-on) state: start un-resolved, resolve in a stagger when .cp-on lands.
    .cp-settled is the SSR/reduced-motion path and never enters this branch. */
 .cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-claim {
   opacity: 0;
   transform: translateY(7px);
 }
 .cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-claim .cp-verdict {
   opacity: 0;
 }
 .cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-glyph {
   transform: scale(0.4);
   opacity: 0;
 }
 .cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-held,
 .cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-decide {
   opacity: 0;
 }
 .cp:not(.cp-settled):not(.cp-on) .cp-held {
   transform: translateY(6px);
 }
 
 .cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(1) {
   transition-delay: 0.05s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(2) {
   transition-delay: 0.55s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(3) {
   transition-delay: 1.1s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(1) .cp-verdict {
   transition-delay: 0.48s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(2) .cp-verdict {
   transition-delay: 1s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-claim:nth-of-type(3) .cp-verdict {
   transition-delay: 1.7s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-held {
   transition-delay: 2.2s;
 }
 .cp.cp-on .cp-decide {
   transition-delay: 2.5s;
 }
 
 @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
   html {
     scroll-behavior: auto;
   }
   .ds-reveal,
   .ds-stagger > * {
     opacity: 1 !important;
     transform: none !important;
     transition: none !important;
   }
   .cp .cp-claim,
   .cp .cp-verdict,
   .cp .cp-held,
   .cp .cp-decide,
   .cp .cp-glyph {
     opacity: 1 !important;
     transform: none !important;
     transition: none !important;
   }
 }
 
 /* ============================================================================
-   Landing-page section primitives (Layer 1 surfaces) — ported from the v3 mockup
+   OXBLOOD LANDING (S2, 2026-07-08) — the editorial scroll-story on the S1 tokens.
+   Spec: the #/ Landing view of whole-site-gallery-v2.html (owner-picked v2). Serif
+   display headlines (scoped to .lp-main), ONE wine accent, hairline-ruled sections,
+   the five-stage flow as a hairline-CONNECTED strip (gate stages emphasised, never
+   numbered 01/02/03). No gradients, no glass, no icon-in-square tiles, radii <= 12px.
+   Replaces the interim lp-* / ds-tech landing block (both were app/page.tsx-only).
+   The CatchPanel keeps its own cp-* block above (SSR-settled + reduced-motion contract).
    ========================================================================== */

-  margin-top: 12px;
-  letter-spacing: -0.002em;
-}
-.ds-tech .tech-body .mono-line .k {
-  color: var(--graphite);
+.lp-strip {
+  margin: 16px 0 0;
+  padding-top: 14px;
+  border-top: 1px solid var(--rule);
+  font-size: 11px;
+  line-height: 1.8;
+  letter-spacing: 0.005em;
+  color: var(--faint);
+  max-width: 82ch;
 }
 
 @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
-  .ds-tech > summary .chev {
+  .lp-btn,
+  .lp-btn .lp-ic,
+  .lp-panel,
+  .lp-step {
     transition: none;
   }
+  /* Codex S2 P3: transitions alone weren't enough — without this the hover
+     transforms still MOVE (instantly) under reduced motion. */
+  .lp-btn.primary:hover,
+  .lp-btn:hover .lp-ic,
+  .lp-panel:hover {
+    transform: none;
+  }
 }
 
 /* ============================================================================
-   THE "LEDGER" DESIGN SYSTEM (owner-picked 2026-07-08) — a Swiss / International-
-   Typographic audit certificate on a gallery-white ground. Scoped to the two real
-   verifier surfaces (/report + /demo, both mount .rpt-wrap); the landing keeps its
-   own system untouched. Reference (adapted, never copied):
-   mockups/design-samples-2026-07-08/sample-1-ledger.html.
-
-   Ultramarine is the ONE accent — SIMULATED tag block · masthead rule · finding
-   index numerals — NEVER on severity. Severity carries its own three marks
-   (error / warn / info), kept apart from the accent. No radii, no shadows, no
-   gradients: the precision of the hairline rules and the spacing IS the design.
+   THE REPORT / DEMO DOCUMENT SYSTEM — re-tokened to "Oxblood" in S3 (2026-07-08).
+   Scoped to the two real verifier surfaces (/report + /demo, both mount .rpt-wrap);
+   the landing keeps its own system. Spec: mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/
+   whole-site-gallery-v2.html (owner-picked). Lineage: the owner-picked "Ledger"
+   audit-certificate STRUCTURE (Swiss / International-Typographic; flat, no radii /
+   shadows / gradients; the precision of the hairline rules and the spacing IS the
+   design) is RETAINED; every token VALUE is remapped to the site Oxblood system.
+
+   S3 re-token: gallery-white ground #FFFFFF → premium off-white #FBFBFD; the cool
+   near-black ink ramp → a warm one; the ONE accent ultramarine → burgundy #8A2233
+   (the token NAME --um is kept so every consumer follows) — SIMULATED tag block ·
+   masthead rule · finding index numerals — NEVER on severity. Severity carries its
+   own three marks (error / warn / info) in warm hues kept perceptually FAR from the
+   burgundy brand, always paired with a square shape + word. Display headings adopt
+   the site serif (var(--serif)) per the mockup's report register; body stays a
+   system sans, receipts stay mono. The SIMULATED banner keeps its unmissable ink
+   band, re-grounded on the warm ink, print-color-adjust intact (test-locked).
+   Contrast recomputed on #FBFBFD (ratios in the token comments below).
    Desktop web + printable (see the @media print block below). rpt-* / dmo-*.
    ========================================================================== */
 .rpt-wrap {
-  /* Ledger tokens — pre-decided; #ffffff is the owner gallery-white modification
-     (the sample used #f9f9f6). Kept as ONE --paper token so text-on-ink surfaces
-     (the banner text, the FAIL flag) follow to #ffffff automatically. */
-  --paper: #ffffff; /* ground + every text-on-ink surface (banner text, verdict flag) */
-  --ink: #101014; /* near-black ink: headline, banner ground, verdict stamp, hard marks */
-  --body: #33333b; /* long-form body text */
-  --graphite: #50505a; /* rail captions, labels, secondary mono */
-  --hairline: #dcdcd4; /* the modular-grid rules (1px, warm-on-white by intent) */
-  --um: #1b3eb8; /* ultramarine — the ONE accent */
-  --sev-err: #a8231b;
-  --sev-warn: #8a5a06;
-  --sev-info: #55555e;
-  /* system font stacks only — no new dependency. Grotesk display + a tabular mono. */
+  /* S3 Oxblood re-token (2026-07-08): the Ledger STRUCTURE is retained; every token
+     VALUE is remapped to the site Oxblood system. Kept as ONE --paper token so every
+     text-on-ink surface (banner text, verdict flag) follows automatically. Contrast
+     recomputed on #fbfbfd — floors: body ≥10:1, graphite ≥7:1, all others ≥4.5:1. */
+  --paper: #fbfbfd; /* premium off-white ground + every text-on-ink surface (banner text, verdict flag) */
+  --ink: #1a1712; /* warm near-black: headline, banner ground, verdict stamp, hard marks — 17.29:1 */
+  --body: #423b38; /* long-form body text — 10.61:1 (sitewide Oxblood body ramp) */
+  --graphite: #564d48; /* rail captions, labels, secondary mono — 7.97:1 */
+  --hairline: rgba(74, 14, 26, 0.14); /* wine-tinted modular-grid rules (site --rule family; visual weight of the old #dcdcd4) */
+  --um: #8a2233; /* the ONE accent — burgundy/oxblood (token NAME kept from ultramarine) — 8.62:1 */
+  --sev-err: #d92d20; /* 4.67:1 — bright/warm, kept perceptually far from the burgundy brand */
+  --sev-warn: #b54708; /* 5.25:1 — amber, far from the brand */
+  --sev-info: #615750; /* warm neutral — 6.80:1 (no info in the fixture; never a brand/alarm hue) */
+  /* system font stacks match the mockup's report register (system sans + system mono);
+     display headings adopt the site serif (var(--serif)). No new dependency. */
   --ledger-sans: "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, "Liberation Sans", sans-serif;
   --ledger-mono: ui-monospace, "SF Mono", SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, "Liberation Mono",
     monospace;
 
   max-width: 1180px;
   margin: 0 auto;
   padding: 48px 64px 120px;
   background: var(--paper);
   color: var(--body);
   font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
   font-size: 15px;
   line-height: 1.55;
   letter-spacing: 0;
 }
 
 /* ---- SIMULATED banner — solid ink band, unmissable, first thing on the page.
    Text uses --paper (#ffffff) on the ink ground; survives print exact. ---- */
 .rpt-sim {
   display: flex;
   align-items: flex-start;
   gap: 22px;
   padding: 20px 24px;
   border: none;
   border-radius: 0;
   background: var(--ink);
   -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
   print-color-adjust: exact;
 }
 .rpt-sim-tag {
   flex: none;
   font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
   font-size: 12px;
   font-weight: 700;
   letter-spacing: 0.16em;
   color: var(--paper);
   background: var(--um);
   padding: 7px 12px;
   border-radius: 0;
   -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
   print-color-adjust: exact;
 }
 .rpt-sim-text {
   min-width: 0;
   margin: 2px 0 0;
   font-size: 13.5px;
   line-height: 1.6;
   color: var(--paper);
   max-width: 92ch;
 }
 
 /* ---- masthead — flush-left, closed by the single 4px ultramarine rule ---- */
 .rpt-head {
   margin-top: 0;
   padding: 44px 0 30px;
   border-bottom: 4px solid var(--um);
 }
 .rpt-eyebrow {
   margin: 0;
   font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
   font-size: 11px;
   font-weight: 700;
   letter-spacing: 0.18em;
   text-transform: uppercase;
   color: var(--graphite);
 }
 .rpt-title {
   margin: 16px 0 0;
-  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
+  /* S3: masthead adopts the site serif per the mockup's report register. */
+  font-family: var(--serif);
   font-size: clamp(34px, 4.4vw, 52px);
-  font-weight: 800;
-  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
-  line-height: 1.02;
+  font-weight: 600;
+  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
+  line-height: 1.05;
   color: var(--ink);
   max-width: 24ch;
   text-wrap: balance;
 }
 .rpt-intro {
   margin: 22px 0 0;
   font-size: 15.5px;
   line-height: 1.6;
   color: var(--body);
   max-width: 68ch;
 }
 
 /* ---- surface toggle — a flush mono tab strip on a hairline; ink underline for
    the active surface (ultramarine is reserved for its three sanctioned roles) -- */
 .rpt-toolbar {
   display: flex;
   gap: 0;
   margin-top: 28px;
   border-bottom: 1px solid var(--hairline);
 }
 .rpt-tab {
   font: inherit;
   font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
   font-size: 12px;
   letter-spacing: 0.04em;
   cursor: pointer;
   padding: 10px 16px 11px;
   border: none;
   border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
   border-radius: 0;
   background: none;
   color: var(--graphite);
   margin-bottom: -1px;
   transition:
     color 0.15s ease,
     border-color 0.15s ease;
 }
 .rpt-tab:first-child {
   padding-left: 0;
 }
 .rpt-tab:hover {
   color: var(--ink);
 }
 .rpt-tab.active {
   color: var(--ink);
   border-bottom-color: var(--ink);
 }
 
 /* ---- the modular-grid armature: 184px caption rail | continuous hairline | body */
 .rpt-sec {
   display: grid;
   grid-template-columns: 184px minmax(0, 1fr);
   border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
 }
 .rpt-toolbar + .rpt-sec {
   border-top: none;
 }
 .rpt-rail {
   margin: 0;
   padding: 30px 20px 30px 0;
   font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
   font-size: 11px;
   font-weight: 700;
   letter-spacing: 0.18em;
   text-transform: uppercase;
   color: var(--graphite);
 }
 .rpt-bodycol {
   min-width: 0;
   border-left: 1px solid var(--hairline);
   padding: 30px 0 34px 40px;
 }
 
 /* ---- verdict — the flag is a solid ink stamp (paper-white text on ink, per the
    --paper token note); the count is mono; the frozen tally sits on its own ruled
    row as a single mono line (it is one text node — never split into squares) ---- */
 .rpt-verdict {
   display: flex;
   align-items: center;
   flex-wrap: wrap;
@@ -1563,158 +1625,159 @@ body {
     padding-left: 0;
   }
   .rpt-mrow {
     grid-template-columns: 1fr;
     gap: 4px;
   }
 }
 
 /* ----- print: one clean report per page, the ink banner preserved on its ground.
    The SIMULATED banner keeps print-color-adjust: exact (on .rpt-sim above) so it
    prints on the ink ground, and stays the first thing on the printed page. ----- */
 @media print {
   nav,
   footer,
   .ds-skip,
   .rpt-toolbar {
     display: none !important;
   }
   html {
     scroll-behavior: auto;
   }
   body {
     font-size: 12px;
   }
   .rpt-wrap {
     max-width: none;
     margin: 0;
     padding: 0;
   }
   @page {
     margin: 14mm;
   }
   /* never split a finding (with its receipts) across a page break */
   .rpt-finding,
   .rpt-sim,
   .rpt-verdict,
   .rpt-meta {
     break-inside: avoid;
   }
   .rpt-head {
     break-after: avoid;
   }
   /* D1 demo: keep each beat whole across page breaks */
   .dmo-beat {
     break-inside: avoid;
   }
 }
 
 /* ============================================================================
    D1 — the one-page verifier demo (/demo). Reuses the report's Ledger system
    (rpt-* classes: banner, masthead, findings, receipts) and adds a beat list.
    Desktop-first, printable. dmo-* namespace.
    ========================================================================== */
 .dmo-meta {
   margin-top: 18px;
 }
 .dmo-beats {
   margin-top: 28px;
   border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
 }
 .dmo-beat {
   padding: 24px 0;
   border-bottom: 1px solid var(--hairline);
 }
 .dmo-beat-head {
   display: flex;
   align-items: baseline;
   gap: 14px;
 }
 .dmo-beat-no {
   flex: none;
   font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
   font-size: 11px;
   font-weight: 700;
   letter-spacing: 0.06em;
   text-transform: uppercase;
   color: var(--graphite);
 }
 .dmo-beat-title {
   margin: 0;
-  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
+  /* S3: beat titles (mockup h3) adopt the site serif per the report register. */
+  font-family: var(--serif);
   font-size: 18px;
   line-height: 1.25;
-  font-weight: 800;
-  letter-spacing: -0.02em;
+  font-weight: 600;
+  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
   color: var(--ink);
 }
 .dmo-beat-plain {
   margin: 12px 0 0;
   font-size: 15px;
   line-height: 1.5;
   color: var(--ink);
   max-width: 72ch;
 }
 .dmo-beat-lines {
   list-style: none;
   margin: 12px 0 0;
   padding: 0;
 }
 .dmo-beat-lines li {
   position: relative;
   padding-left: 16px;
   font-size: 13px;
   line-height: 1.55;
   color: var(--body);
 }
 .dmo-beat-lines li::before {
   content: "·";
   position: absolute;
   left: 2px;
   color: var(--graphite);
 }
 /* demo verdict chips — flat severity marks (no pill / radius), consistent with
    the report's typographic severity */
 .dmo-verdicts {
   display: flex;
   flex-wrap: wrap;
   gap: 10px 24px;
   margin-top: 14px;
 }
 .dmo-verdict {
   display: inline-flex;
   align-items: center;
   gap: 8px;
   padding: 0;
   border: none;
   border-radius: 0;
   background: none;
   font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
   font-size: 11px;
   font-weight: 500;
   letter-spacing: 0.02em;
 }
 .dmo-verdict::before {
   content: "";
   width: 9px;
   height: 9px;
   flex: none;
   -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
   print-color-adjust: exact;
 }
 .dmo-verdict.ok {
   color: var(--ink);
 }
 .dmo-verdict.ok::before {
   background: var(--ink);
 }
 .dmo-verdict.flag {
   color: var(--sev-err);
   font-weight: 700;
 }
 .dmo-verdict.flag::before {
   background: var(--sev-err);
 }
 .dmo-findings {
   margin-top: 16px;
   border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
 }

   169	describe("print fidelity — the SIMULATED banner survives print on its ink ground", () => {
   170	  // The report is a printable one-pager. Chrome/Safari drop background colours
   171	  // when printing unless color-adjust is forced exact — without it the SIMULATED
   172	  // banner (paper text on an ink ground) would print as invisible white-on-white,
   173	  // silently stripping the honesty label from a printed copy. No existing test
   174	  // guards this; this is the print-checklist assertion for the Ledger restyle.

  1194	
  1195	/* ---- SIMULATED banner — solid ink band, unmissable, first thing on the page.
  1196	   Text uses --paper (#ffffff) on the ink ground; survives print exact. ---- */
  1197	.rpt-sim {
  1198	  display: flex;
  1199	  align-items: flex-start;
  1200	  gap: 22px;
  1201	  padding: 20px 24px;
  1202	  border: none;
  1203	  border-radius: 0;
  1204	  background: var(--ink);
  1205	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1206	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1207	}
  1208	.rpt-sim-tag {
  1209	  flex: none;
  1210	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1211	  font-size: 12px;
  1212	  font-weight: 700;
  1213	  letter-spacing: 0.16em;
  1214	  color: var(--paper);
  1215	  background: var(--um);
  1216	  padding: 7px 12px;
  1217	  border-radius: 0;
  1218	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1219	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1220	}
  1221	.rpt-sim-text {
  1222	  min-width: 0;
  1223	  margin: 2px 0 0;
  1224	  font-size: 13.5px;
  1225	  line-height: 1.6;
  1226	  color: var(--paper);
  1227	  max-width: 92ch;
  1228	}
  1229	
  1230	/* ---- masthead — flush-left, closed by the single 4px ultramarine rule ---- */
  1231	.rpt-head {
  1232	  margin-top: 0;
  1233	  padding: 44px 0 30px;
  1234	  border-bottom: 4px solid var(--um);
  1235	}
  1236	.rpt-eyebrow {
  1237	  margin: 0;
  1238	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1239	  font-size: 11px;
  1240	  font-weight: 700;
  1241	  letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  1242	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1243	  color: var(--graphite);
  1244	}
  1245	.rpt-title {
  1246	  margin: 16px 0 0;
  1247	  /* S3: masthead adopts the site serif per the mockup's report register. */
  1248	  font-family: var(--serif);
  1249	  font-size: clamp(34px, 4.4vw, 52px);
  1250	  font-weight: 600;
  1251	  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
  1252	  line-height: 1.05;
  1253	  color: var(--ink);
  1254	  max-width: 24ch;
  1255	  text-wrap: balance;
  1256	}
  1257	.rpt-intro {
  1258	  margin: 22px 0 0;
  1259	  font-size: 15.5px;
  1260	  line-height: 1.6;
  1261	  color: var(--body);
  1262	  max-width: 68ch;
  1263	}
  1264	
  1265	/* ---- surface toggle — a flush mono tab strip on a hairline; ink underline for
  1266	   the active surface (ultramarine is reserved for its three sanctioned roles) -- */
  1267	.rpt-toolbar {
  1268	  display: flex;
  1269	  gap: 0;
  1270	  margin-top: 28px;
  1271	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1272	}
  1273	.rpt-tab {
  1274	  font: inherit;
  1275	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1276	  font-size: 12px;
  1277	  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  1278	  cursor: pointer;
  1279	  padding: 10px 16px 11px;
  1280	  border: none;
  1281	  border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
  1282	  border-radius: 0;
  1283	  background: none;
  1284	  color: var(--graphite);
  1285	  margin-bottom: -1px;
  1286	  transition:
  1287	    color 0.15s ease,
  1288	    border-color 0.15s ease;
  1289	}
  1290	.rpt-tab:first-child {
  1291	  padding-left: 0;
  1292	}
  1293	.rpt-tab:hover {
  1294	  color: var(--ink);
  1295	}
  1296	.rpt-tab.active {
  1297	  color: var(--ink);
  1298	  border-bottom-color: var(--ink);
  1299	}
  1300	
  1301	/* ---- the modular-grid armature: 184px caption rail | continuous hairline | body */
  1302	.rpt-sec {
  1303	  display: grid;
  1304	  grid-template-columns: 184px minmax(0, 1fr);
  1305	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1306	}
  1307	.rpt-toolbar + .rpt-sec {
  1308	  border-top: none;
  1309	}
  1310	.rpt-rail {
  1311	  margin: 0;
  1312	  padding: 30px 20px 30px 0;
  1313	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1314	  font-size: 11px;
  1315	  font-weight: 700;
  1316	  letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  1317	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1318	  color: var(--graphite);
  1319	}
  1320	.rpt-bodycol {
  1321	  min-width: 0;
  1322	  border-left: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1323	  padding: 30px 0 34px 40px;
  1324	}
  1325	
  1326	/* ---- verdict — the flag is a solid ink stamp (paper-white text on ink, per the
  1327	   --paper token note); the count is mono; the frozen tally sits on its own ruled
  1328	   row as a single mono line (it is one text node — never split into squares) ---- */
  1329	.rpt-verdict {
  1330	  display: flex;
  1331	  align-items: center;
  1332	  flex-wrap: wrap;
  1333	  column-gap: 22px;
  1334	  row-gap: 0;
  1335	  margin: 0;
  1336	  padding: 0;
  1337	  border: none;
  1338	  border-radius: 0;
  1339	  background: none;
  1340	}
  1341	.rpt-verdict-flag {
  1342	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1343	  font-size: 30px;
  1344	  font-weight: 800;
  1345	  letter-spacing: -0.02em;
  1346	  line-height: 1;
  1347	  color: var(--paper);
  1348	  background: var(--ink);
  1349	  padding: 11px 18px;
  1350	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1351	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1352	}
  1353	.rpt-verdict-count {
  1354	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1355	  font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  1356	  font-size: 16px;
  1357	  font-weight: 700;
  1358	  color: var(--ink);
  1359	}
  1360	.rpt-verdict-tally {
  1361	  width: 100%;
  1362	  margin: 20px 0 0;
  1363	  padding-top: 14px;
  1364	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1365	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1366	  font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  1367	  font-size: 12.5px;
  1368	  color: var(--graphite);
  1369	}
  1370	
  1371	/* ---- meta ledger ---- */
  1372	.rpt-meta {
  1373	  margin: 0;
  1374	  border: none;
  1375	  border-radius: 0;
  1376	  overflow: visible;
  1377	}
  1378	.rpt-mrow {
  1379	  display: grid;
  1380	  grid-template-columns: 170px minmax(0, 1fr);
  1381	  gap: 24px;
  1382	  padding: 12px 0;
  1383	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1384	}
  1385	.rpt-mrow:first-child {
  1386	  border-top: none;
  1387	  padding-top: 0;
  1388	}
  1389	.rpt-mrow:last-child {
  1390	  padding-bottom: 0;
  1391	}
  1392	.rpt-mrow dt {
  1393	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1394	  font-size: 11px;
  1395	  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  1396	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1397	  color: var(--graphite);
  1398	}
  1399	.rpt-mrow dd {
  1400	  margin: 0;
  1401	  min-width: 0;
  1402	  font-size: 13.5px;
  1403	  color: var(--ink);
  1404	}
  1405	
  1406	/* ---- findings — numbered rows: the plain line leads, four ruled mono receipt
  1407	   columns follow; index numerals wear the ultramarine accent ---- */
  1408	.rpt-findings {
  1409	  list-style: none;
  1410	  margin: 0;
  1411	  padding: 0;
  1412	  border-top: none;
  1413	}
  1414	.rpt-finding {
  1415	  padding: 22px 0 24px;
  1416	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1417	  border-bottom: none;
  1418	}
  1419	.rpt-finding:first-child {
  1420	  padding-top: 0;
  1421	  border-top: none;
  1422	}
  1423	.rpt-finding-lead {
  1424	  display: grid;
  1425	  grid-template-columns: 56px minmax(0, 1fr) auto;
  1426	  column-gap: 18px;
  1427	  align-items: baseline;
  1428	}
  1429	.rpt-idx {
  1430	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1431	  font-size: 21px;
  1432	  font-weight: 800;
  1433	  letter-spacing: -0.02em;
  1434	  font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  1435	  color: var(--um);
  1436	  flex: none;
  1437	}
  1438	.rpt-plain {
  1439	  margin: 0;
  1440	  flex: initial;
  1441	  font-size: 16px;
  1442	  line-height: 1.45;
  1443	  font-weight: 600;
  1444	  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
  1445	  color: var(--ink);
  1446	  max-width: 74ch;
  1447	}
  1448	/* severity — typographic weight + a 9px square mark (no pill, no radius). The
  1449	   square is a ::before so no markup changes and the frozen text nodes stay put. */
  1450	.rpt-sev {
  1451	  display: inline-flex;
  1452	  align-items: center;
  1453	  gap: 8px;
  1454	  flex: none;
  1455	  padding: 0;
  1456	  border: none;
  1457	  border-radius: 0;
  1458	  background: none;
  1459	  text-transform: none;
  1460	  white-space: nowrap;
  1461	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1462	  font-size: 11px;
  1463	  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  1464	}
  1465	.rpt-sev::before {
  1466	  content: "";
  1467	  width: 9px;
  1468	  height: 9px;
  1469	  flex: none;
  1470	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1471	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1472	}
  1473	.rpt-sev.error {
  1474	  color: var(--sev-err);
  1475	  font-weight: 700;
  1476	}
  1477	.rpt-sev.error::before {
  1478	  background: var(--sev-err);
  1479	}
  1480	.rpt-sev.warn {
  1481	  color: var(--sev-warn);
  1482	  font-weight: 500;
  1483	}
  1484	.rpt-sev.warn::before {
  1485	  background: var(--sev-warn);
  1486	}
  1487	.rpt-sev.info {
  1488	  color: var(--sev-info);
  1489	  font-weight: 400;
  1490	}
  1491	.rpt-sev.info::before {
  1492	  background: var(--sev-info);
  1493	}
  1494	
  1495	/* the four receipts — held in hairline-separated mono columns, aligned under the
  1496	   plain line (past the 56px numeral column + 18px gap) */
  1497	.rpt-receipts {
  1498	  display: grid;
  1499	  grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 2.1fr) minmax(0, 1fr) minmax(0, 1.35fr) minmax(0, 0.9fr);
  1500	  gap: 0;
  1501	  margin: 16px 0 0 74px;
  1502	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1503	}
  1504	.rpt-rc {
  1505	  display: block;
  1506	  min-width: 0;
  1507	  padding: 11px 18px 0 0;
  1508	}
  1509	.rpt-rc + .rpt-rc {
  1510	  border-left: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1511	  padding-left: 18px;
  1512	}
  1513	.rpt-rc dt {
  1514	  margin: 0 0 5px;
  1515	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1516	  font-size: 10px;
  1517	  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  1518	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1519	  color: var(--graphite);
  1520	}
  1521	.rpt-rc dd {
  1522	  margin: 0;
  1523	  min-width: 0;
  1524	}
  1525	.rpt-mono {
  1526	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1527	  font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  1528	  font-size: 12px;
  1529	  color: var(--ink);
  1530	  overflow-wrap: anywhere;
  1531	}
  1532	/* meta prose sub — grotesk secondary (the plain-language explanation) */
  1533	.rpt-rc-sub {
  1534	  display: block;
  1535	  margin-top: 3px;
  1536	  font-family: var(--ledger-sans);
  1537	  font-size: 12.5px;
  1538	  line-height: 1.55;
  1539	  color: var(--graphite);
  1540	}
  1541	/* findings claim-value sub — mono tabular (the technical receipt value) */
  1542	.rpt-mono-sub {
  1543	  display: block;
  1544	  margin-top: 3px;
  1545	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1546	  font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
  1547	  font-size: 11.5px;
  1548	  line-height: 1.55;
  1549	  color: var(--graphite);
  1550	  overflow-wrap: anywhere;
  1551	}
  1552	
  1553	/* ---- footer — honesty paragraph on the same armature (report: empty rail;
  1554	   demo: a plain ruled block, no rail grid) ---- */
  1555	.rpt-foot {
  1556	  margin: 0;
  1557	  padding: 0;
  1558	  border-top: none;
  1559	}
  1560	.rpt-sec.rpt-foot .rpt-bodycol {
  1561	  padding-bottom: 0;
  1562	}
  1563	.dmo-wrap .rpt-foot {
  1564	  margin-top: 22px;
  1565	  padding-top: 16px;
  1566	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1567	}
  1568	.rpt-foot p {
  1569	  margin: 0;
  1570	  font-size: 12.5px;
  1571	  line-height: 1.65;
  1572	  color: var(--graphite);
  1573	  max-width: 78ch;
  1574	}
  1575	
  1576	/* ---- collapse below the desktop measure — the grid discipline folds to one
  1577	   column (the surface targets desktop web; this only guards narrow viewports) -- */
  1578	@media (max-width: 900px) {
  1579	  .rpt-wrap {
  1580	    padding: 28px 24px 96px;
  1581	  }
  1582	  .rpt-sec {
  1583	    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  1584	  }
  1585	  .rpt-rail {
  1586	    padding: 22px 0 0;
  1587	  }
  1588	  .rpt-bodycol {
  1589	    border-left: none;
  1590	    padding: 14px 0 28px;
  1591	  }
  1592	  .rpt-finding-lead {
  1593	    grid-template-columns: 44px minmax(0, 1fr);
  1594	  }
  1595	  .rpt-sev {
  1596	    grid-column: 2;
  1597	    justify-self: start;
  1598	    margin-top: 8px;
  1599	  }
  1600	  .rpt-receipts {
  1601	    grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) minmax(0, 1fr);
  1602	    margin-left: 0;
  1603	  }
  1604	  .rpt-rc {
  1605	    padding-top: 12px;
  1606	  }
  1607	  .rpt-rc:nth-child(odd) {
  1608	    border-left: none;
  1609	    padding-left: 0;
  1610	  }
  1611	  .rpt-sim {
  1612	    flex-direction: column;
  1613	    gap: 12px;
  1614	  }
  1615	}
  1616	@media (max-width: 560px) {
  1617	  .rpt-wrap {
  1618	    padding: 20px;
  1619	  }
  1620	  .rpt-receipts {
  1621	    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  1622	  }
  1623	  .rpt-rc + .rpt-rc {
  1624	    border-left: none;
  1625	    padding-left: 0;
  1626	  }
  1627	  .rpt-mrow {
  1628	    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  1629	    gap: 4px;
  1630	  }
  1631	}
  1632	
  1633	/* ----- print: one clean report per page, the ink banner preserved on its ground.
  1634	   The SIMULATED banner keeps print-color-adjust: exact (on .rpt-sim above) so it
  1635	   prints on the ink ground, and stays the first thing on the printed page. ----- */
  1636	@media print {
  1637	  nav,
  1638	  footer,
  1639	  .ds-skip,
  1640	  .rpt-toolbar {
  1641	    display: none !important;
  1642	  }
  1643	  html {
  1644	    scroll-behavior: auto;
  1645	  }
  1646	  body {
  1647	    font-size: 12px;
  1648	  }
  1649	  .rpt-wrap {
  1650	    max-width: none;
  1651	    margin: 0;
  1652	    padding: 0;
  1653	  }
  1654	  @page {
  1655	    margin: 14mm;
  1656	  }
  1657	  /* never split a finding (with its receipts) across a page break */
  1658	  .rpt-finding,
  1659	  .rpt-sim,
  1660	  .rpt-verdict,
  1661	  .rpt-meta {
  1662	    break-inside: avoid;
  1663	  }
  1664	  .rpt-head {
  1665	    break-after: avoid;
  1666	  }
  1667	  /* D1 demo: keep each beat whole across page breaks */
  1668	  .dmo-beat {
  1669	    break-inside: avoid;
  1670	  }
  1671	}
  1672	
  1673	/* ============================================================================
  1674	   D1 — the one-page verifier demo (/demo). Reuses the report's Ledger system
  1675	   (rpt-* classes: banner, masthead, findings, receipts) and adds a beat list.
  1676	   Desktop-first, printable. dmo-* namespace.
  1677	   ========================================================================== */
  1678	.dmo-meta {
  1679	  margin-top: 18px;
  1680	}
  1681	.dmo-beats {
  1682	  margin-top: 28px;
  1683	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1684	}
  1685	.dmo-beat {
  1686	  padding: 24px 0;
  1687	  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1688	}
  1689	.dmo-beat-head {
  1690	  display: flex;
  1691	  align-items: baseline;
  1692	  gap: 14px;
  1693	}
  1694	.dmo-beat-no {
  1695	  flex: none;
  1696	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1697	  font-size: 11px;
  1698	  font-weight: 700;
  1699	  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  1700	  text-transform: uppercase;
  1701	  color: var(--graphite);
  1702	}
  1703	.dmo-beat-title {
  1704	  margin: 0;
  1705	  /* S3: beat titles (mockup h3) adopt the site serif per the report register. */
  1706	  font-family: var(--serif);
  1707	  font-size: 18px;
  1708	  line-height: 1.25;
  1709	  font-weight: 600;
  1710	  letter-spacing: -0.012em;
  1711	  color: var(--ink);
  1712	}
  1713	.dmo-beat-plain {
  1714	  margin: 12px 0 0;
  1715	  font-size: 15px;
  1716	  line-height: 1.5;
  1717	  color: var(--ink);
  1718	  max-width: 72ch;
  1719	}
  1720	.dmo-beat-lines {
  1721	  list-style: none;
  1722	  margin: 12px 0 0;
  1723	  padding: 0;
  1724	}
  1725	.dmo-beat-lines li {
  1726	  position: relative;
  1727	  padding-left: 16px;
  1728	  font-size: 13px;
  1729	  line-height: 1.55;
  1730	  color: var(--body);
  1731	}
  1732	.dmo-beat-lines li::before {
  1733	  content: "·";
  1734	  position: absolute;
  1735	  left: 2px;
  1736	  color: var(--graphite);
  1737	}
  1738	/* demo verdict chips — flat severity marks (no pill / radius), consistent with
  1739	   the report's typographic severity */
  1740	.dmo-verdicts {
  1741	  display: flex;
  1742	  flex-wrap: wrap;
  1743	  gap: 10px 24px;
  1744	  margin-top: 14px;
  1745	}
  1746	.dmo-verdict {
  1747	  display: inline-flex;
  1748	  align-items: center;
  1749	  gap: 8px;
  1750	  padding: 0;
  1751	  border: none;
  1752	  border-radius: 0;
  1753	  background: none;
  1754	  font-family: var(--ledger-mono);
  1755	  font-size: 11px;
  1756	  font-weight: 500;
  1757	  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
  1758	}
  1759	.dmo-verdict::before {
  1760	  content: "";
  1761	  width: 9px;
  1762	  height: 9px;
  1763	  flex: none;
  1764	  -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact;
  1765	  print-color-adjust: exact;
  1766	}
  1767	.dmo-verdict.ok {
  1768	  color: var(--ink);
  1769	}
  1770	.dmo-verdict.ok::before {
  1771	  background: var(--ink);
  1772	}
  1773	.dmo-verdict.flag {
  1774	  color: var(--sev-err);
  1775	  font-weight: 700;
  1776	}
  1777	.dmo-verdict.flag::before {
  1778	  background: var(--sev-err);
  1779	}
  1780	.dmo-findings {
  1781	  margin-top: 16px;
  1782	  border-top: 1px solid var(--hairline);
  1783	}

   102	
   103	export function ReportView() {
   104	  const [surface, setSurface] = useState<SurfaceKey>("acp");
   105	  const active = SURFACES[surface];
   106	  const report = active.report;
   107	
   108	  return (
   109	    <main className="rpt-wrap" id="report">

   139	            aria-selected={surface === key}

   144	          </button>
   145	        ))}

289: {plain:'The sale price 28.50 is higher than the regular price 27.50 — a "sale" that costs more.',sev:"error",claimId:"item-001-v3#invariants",src:"acp-feed",field:"invariants",val:"price 27.50 · sale 28.50",ref:"item-001-v3",rule:"LST-PRICE-SALE-GT",cls:"price"},
290: {plain:"The served price 12.00 does not match the catalog price 10.00.",sev:"error",claimId:"item-006-v1#price.amount",src:"acp-feed",field:"price.amount",val:"12.00",ref:"item-006-v1",rule:"LST-PRICE-VALUE",cls:"price"},

412:    <div class="panel reveal"><div style="font-weight:600;font-size:15px;margin-bottom:6px;font-family:var(--serif)">It's tuned to hold, not to over-block.</div><p class="lede" style="font-size:13.5px">A false hold costs a glance; a shipped falsehood costs trust.</p></div>
413:    <div class="panel reveal"><div style="font-weight:600;font-size:15px;margin-bottom:6px;font-family:var(--serif)">The exact check underneath is locked.</div><p class="lede" style="font-size:13.5px">The deterministic comparison is fixed and auditable — it can't drift.</p></div>
429:  <p class="lede techlead" style="margin-top:10px"><b style="color:var(--ink-2)">Technically:</b> deterministic risk + blocker triage → bounded, schema-constrained LLM drafting → a claims-gatekeeper that ties every declared claim to the merchant's own data → an eval harness over the output → a human approval gate with an audit trail.</p>
438:    <div class="stat"><div class="v">REPLAY</div><div class="l">Mode</div><div class="s">demo makes no live calls</div></div>

466:function vReport(){const err=FINDINGS.filter(f=>f.sev==='error').length,warn=FINDINGS.filter(f=>f.sev==='warn').length;
468:  <div class="sim" role="note"><span class="sim-tag">${icon('alert')}SIMULATED</span><span class="sim-text">${BANNER}</span></div>
469:  <p class="eyebrow">${icon('receipt')} Verifier report · listings truth check</p>
471:  <p class="lede">A serving copy of a menu — the version an AI shopping assistant would read — checked line by line against the restaurant's own system-of-record. Below is every difference the checker caught: each in plain words first, then the exact receipts. Deterministic and $0, no AI calls on this path — the same input always gives this same report.</p>
485:  <div class="rail"><h3></h3><div><p class="lede" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--muted)">Every row above carries its four receipts — the claim, the catalog row it was checked against, the rule it broke, and how severe it is. No language model runs in this verifier — the comparison is exact, deterministic logic. Simulated prototype, run on demand — not a live service. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.</p></div></div>
494:  <div class="sim" role="note"><span class="sim-tag">${icon('alert')}SIMULATED</span><span class="sim-text">${BANNER}</span></div>
495:  <p class="eyebrow">${icon('eye')} Verifier demo · listings truth check</p>
497:  <p class="lede">The comparison is exact, deterministic logic — no language model runs in this demo, and no live platform is touched.</p>
499:   <div class="meta-row"><dt>actor</dt><dd>spec-faithful demonstration actor — simulated</dd></div>
505:  <p class="lede" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--muted);margin-top:26px">The agent read only the published feed and never saw the restaurant's records; the verifier checked that same feed against those records and cited every catch with its four receipts. No language model runs in this demo — the comparison is exact, deterministic logic. Simulated prototype, run on demand — not a live service, no real platform access. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.</p>
512:  <p class="lede techlead" style="margin-top:10px"><b style="color:var(--ink-2)">Technically:</b> deterministic graders over the draft contract (structure · state-consistency · policy · no-leakage). Their teeth are proven by paired corrupted-record tests (a grader that can't fail is theater) — including on the recorded real-Gemini drafts, where no-leakage catches a raw enum / risk-level leak the other dimensions missed.</p>
513:  <div class="note warnish" style="margin-top:16px">These corpus scores grade the <b>deterministic stub</b> output. The same graders also scored a <b>recorded real Gemini run</b> — shown below (key-gated, $0.0042 spent) — so this stays honest about real output. The public <b>demo itself makes no live calls</b>.</div>
522:  <p class="lede" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-2);margin-bottom:14px">A <b>frozen recording</b> of a local Gemini API run (one merchant per blocker). The public demo does <b>not</b> re-run or independently verify it (REPLAY-only, zero spend) — reproduce it yourself with your own key. Total cost: <span class="mono">$${LIVE.total.toFixed(4)}</span> (cap $5).</p>
523:  <div class="note" style="margin-bottom:14px"><div class="kicker" style="color:var(--muted);margin-bottom:8px">What the live run showed (honest)</div><ul style="margin:0;padding-left:18px;font-size:12.5px;color:var(--ink-2);line-height:1.7">${LIVE.honest.map(h=>`<li>${esc(h)}</li>`).join('')}</ul></div>
533:  <p class="lede plainlead"><b>In plain terms:</b> what the demo routes and tracks for an activation team — how many stalled merchants get a claim-checked nudge, how many are held for a human, and what's blocking them.</p>
534:  <div class="note warnish" style="margin-top:16px">Figures are <b>simulated</b> over the hybrid demo set (fictional names, synthetic activation state) — illustrative of the workflow, <b>not activation, revenue, or reactivation outcomes</b>.</div>
549:  <p class="lede" style="font-size:13px;color:var(--muted);margin-top:8px">Run executed deterministically at <span class="mono" style="color:var(--ink-2)">2026-07-08T00:00:00Z</span> (mode acp). Open a merchant for its full step-by-step trail.</p>
552:     <td class="mono" style="font-size:12px;color:var(--ink-2)">${m.risk} · ${esc(m.blocker)}</td>
555:     <td style="font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-2)">${m.status}</td>
563:  <p class="lede plainlead"><b>In plain terms:</b> the live-drafting path is budget-guarded, so a run can't quietly exceed the cap. Spend is computed from real reported tokens against a pinned price list, and a fail-closed hard stop blocks any call that would cross it.</p>
572:  <ul style="margin:8px 0 0;padding-left:18px;font-size:13px;color:var(--ink-2);line-height:1.8">
573:    <li>Cost = real API-reported tokens × a pinned, versioned price table (not an estimate).</li>
581:   ${PRICES.map(p=>`<tr><td class="mono" style="font-size:12.5px">${esc(p[0])}</td><td class="mono" style="color:var(--ink-2)">$${p[1].toFixed(2)}</td><td class="mono" style="color:var(--ink-2)">$${p[2].toFixed(2)}</td></tr>`).join('')}

    63	function Beat({ beat, ordinal }: { beat: DemoBeat; ordinal: number }) {
    64	  return (
    65	    <section className="dmo-beat" aria-label={beat.title}>
    66	      <div className="dmo-beat-head">
    67	        <span className="dmo-beat-no">Beat {ordinal}</span>
    68	        <h2 className="dmo-beat-title">{beat.title}</h2>
    69	      </div>
    70	      {/* C4: the plain line leads the beat. */}
    71	      <p className="dmo-beat-plain">{beat.plain}</p>
    72	      <ul className="dmo-beat-lines">
    73	        {beat.lines.map((line, i) => (
    74	          <li key={i}>{line}</li>
    75	        ))}
    76	      </ul>
    77	      {beat.verdicts && beat.verdicts.length > 0 ? (
    78	        <div className="dmo-verdicts">
    79	          {beat.verdicts.map((v, i) => (
    80	            <span key={i} className={`dmo-verdict ${v.ok ? "ok" : "flag"}`}>
    81	              {v.label}
    82	            </span>
    83	          ))}
    84	        </div>
    85	      ) : null}
    86	      {beat.findings && beat.findings.length > 0 ? (
    87	        <ol className="rpt-findings dmo-findings">
    88	          {beat.findings.map((f, i) => (
    89	            <Receipts key={`${f.claimId}:${f.ruleId}`} finding={f} index={i} />
    90	          ))}
    91	        </ol>
    92	      ) : null}
    93	    </section>
    94	  );
    95	}
    96	
    97	export function DemoView() {
    98	  return (
    99	    <main className="rpt-wrap dmo-wrap" id="demo">
   100	      {/* C10: the SIMULATED label — unmissable, survives print. */}
   101	      <div className="rpt-sim" role="note">
   102	        <span className="rpt-sim-tag">SIMULATED</span>
   103	        <span className="rpt-sim-text">{DEMO_SIMULATED_BANNER}</span>
   104	      </div>
   105	
   106	      <header className="rpt-head">
   107	        <p className="rpt-eyebrow">Verifier demo · listings truth check</p>
   108	        {/* C7: the verbatim demo claim is the only headline. */}
   109	        <h1 className="rpt-title">{transcript.claim}</h1>
   110	        <p className="rpt-intro">{DEMO_SUBHEAD}</p>
   111	        <dl className="rpt-meta dmo-meta">
   112	          <div className="rpt-mrow">
   113	            <dt>actor</dt>
   114	            <dd>{transcript.actorLabel}</dd>
   115	          </div>
   116	          <div className="rpt-mrow">
   117	            <dt>spec version</dt>
   118	            <dd className="rpt-mono">{transcript.specVersion}</dd>
   119	          </div>
   120	          <div className="rpt-mrow">
   121	            <dt>data</dt>
   122	            <dd className="rpt-mono">simulated: {String(transcript.simulated)}</dd>
   123	          </div>
   124	        </dl>
   125	      </header>
   126	
   127	      <div className="dmo-beats">
   128	        {transcript.beats.map((beat, i) => (
   129	          <Beat key={beat.id} beat={beat} ordinal={i + 1} />
   130	        ))}
   131	      </div>
   132	
   133	      <footer className="rpt-foot">
   134	        <p>
   135	          The agent read only the published feed and never saw the restaurant&rsquo;s records; the
   136	          verifier checked that same feed against those records and cited every catch with its four
   137	          receipts. No language model runs in this demo &mdash; the comparison is exact,
   138	          deterministic logic. Simulated prototype, run on demand &mdash; not a live service, no
   139	          real platform access. Human-led, AI-assisted, professionally reviewed.
   140	        </p>
   141	      </footer>
   142	    </main>
   143	  );
   144	}

The local evidence so far supports C1 and the print assertions: the four TSX/page files have an empty diff, and the print test still scopes directly to the `.rpt-sim` rule block. I also found one likely non-blocking issue candidate: a few comments inside the S3 CSS still say `#ffffff`/“ultramarine” after the token remap; I’m separating that from runtime behavior.

docs/task-log.md:7:- **Part A — web view leg (C4):** `/report` page — `app/report/page.tsx` (server, metadata) → `components/report/ReportView.tsx` (client, surface toggle ACP/UCP) → renders the COMMITTED golden fixture reports (`expected-report.{acp,ucp}.json`, statically imported, bundled). Plain-words line LEADS each finding; the four C2 receipts (claim · reference-row · rule · severity) always visible; header pins specVersion + matchingMode (plain-decoded) + an unmissable SIMULATED banner (C10). Route prerenders `○ Static`; $0, zero LLM, zero network. Pure transform in `lib/packs/listings/report-view.ts`.
docs/task-log.md:1528:| 2026-07-08 | Seventeenth session — DESIGN SAMPLES (owner word: "give 3 to 5 design samples using all the skills and subagents based the context."): design-shotgun over the `/report` surface — five parallel frontend-specialist subagents, one direction each (1 Ledger Swiss audit certificate · 2 Console forensic terminal · 3 Broadsheet serif investigation · 4 Control Room audit-dashboard UI · 5 Dossier evidence case-file), all rendering the SAME committed golden fixture (FAIL · 16 findings · 11/5/0) under the honesty gates (verbatim SIMULATED banner, plain-line-first + four receipts, real tally, footer paragraph, no banned framing, zero external requests). DEVIATION: all five builders died at their FINAL step on the shared subagent seat limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 9:10am (America/New_York)") AFTER their files landed; safety classifier down for their review → Fable-equivalence review executed INLINE (machine checks: banner/footer verbatim ×5, 16/16 findings ×5, tally recomputed-correct ×5, banned-framing 0, external URLs 0, fragment format ×5 — ALL PASS); owner confirmed the seat reset afterward. Durable copies + verification record: mockups/design-samples-2026-07-08/ (+README); published as 5 claude.ai artifacts. Recommendation on record (not a decision): #1 Ledger primary · #4 Control Room alternative · #2 Console personality. NO product code; verify baseline 944+6 stands. Skills/tools: artifact-design skill, frontend-specialist ×5, Artifact publisher. | Awaiting the owner's design pick (implementation prompt in HANDOFF) |

docs/plan-whole-site-redesign.md:16:The whole-site rewrite **supersedes** the interim Ledger `/report` styling. Sitewide ground = **`#FBFBFD`** (v2 research pick). This changes the already-implemented `/report` + `/demo` from the committed Ledger `--paper #FFFFFF` — that is intended (the whole-site system replaces it). If the owner instead wants the sitewide ground held at `#FFFFFF`, that is a one-token override at planning — flag before S1.
docs/plan-whole-site-redesign.md:19:Unlike the Ledger `/report` restyle (which was CONTENT-FROZEN), this redesign **changes content** (story-arc narrative) per the owner's "layout + content" directive. But the **honesty invariants stay byte-frozen**:
docs/plan-whole-site-redesign.md:34:- **S3 — Report + Demo (honesty-critical):** `report/page.tsx` + `ReportView.tsx`, `demo/page.tsx` + `DemoView.tsx` — re-skin to Oxblood, **supersede the Ledger styling**, reconcile ground to `#FBFBFD`; preserve SIMULATED banner byte-verbatim + footer + 16 findings 11/5/0 + the print stylesheet; **consciously update the +3 print-fidelity assertions + report/demo view tests red-green** (they were written for the Ledger system).

Ruling applied: sample #6 revised in place to a light ground (the research pass's equally-legitimate "stark-white" pole; coheres with the implemented gallery-white Ledger surfaces); content byte-unchanged (the gated copy deck); palette re-derived for ≥4.5:1 contrast on white; treated as a STANDING preference for world-facing surfaces going forward (any future dark direction needs the owner's explicit word). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-08) | Direct owner word on a delivered artifact; light ground also matches the picked product identity | This row; sample-6-instrument.html token comment; owner message 2026-07-08 |

Content byte-unchanged (gated deck). Scoped to sample 6 only — the implemented /report Ledger ground (#FFFFFF, the owner's earlier pick) stands. | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-08) | Direct owner word on the delivered artifact; refines the standing light-ground preference with a premium-white treatment | This row; sample-6 token comment; owner message 2026-07-08 |
docs/decision-log.md:132:| 2026-07-08 | **WHOLE-SITE REDESIGN — DIRECTION + IDENTITY + STEPS FIXED (owner, eighteenth session cont.; AskUserQuestion structured answers, verbatim selections).** The whole-site redesign (all 10 routes + shell + content) was delivered mockup-first in `mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/` (owner directives + verbatim quotes durably recorded in that dir's README/build-spec/story-arc: the `/claude-os` whole-site directive "ultramodern Premium UI, gallery white, redesign whole website all layout + content, without training knowledge/memory" → the v2 revision "remove the grid · keep gallery white · dark red/maroon/burgundy · UI+MOTION+ICONS+FONTS · story-arc narrative · simple in depth & breadth, domain-expert · fullest capabilities"). This turn the three writer-flagged open items were put to the owner and answered: **① Direction = "Oxblood v2 (burgundy)"** (`whole-site-gallery-v2.html` — premium off-white #FBFBFD ground, ONE burgundy accent #8A2233, grid removed, serif-display+mono, custom monoline SVG icons, scroll-motion, story-arc content); **② Identity = "Unify → Commerce Truth Audit"** (retire the page-title "ActivationOps" + console "Curbside Commons" from the site; the S-11 real-brand pick, e.g. Plumbline, stays a separate open owner act and would rename later); **③ TOTAL_STEPS = 5 confirmed** (landing pipeline copy correct as built). This FIRES the gated product-code implementation (rewrite `app/globals.css` + all pages/components + content, under the full per-slice gate). **Ground reconciliation flagged, not silently resolved:** v2's #FBFBFD vs the already-implemented `/report` `--paper #FFFFFF` (decision-log 2026-07-08 Ledger pick) — to be reconciled at implementation planning (the whole-site rewrite supersedes the interim Ledger `/report` styling, so #FBFBFD becomes the sitewide ground unless the owner says otherwise). Build routes to Opus lane, judged at the Fable-equivalence bar (design-quality standard, this date); deploy stays a SEPARATE owner act (design-first ruling 2026-07-03). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-08) | Owner answered the three surfaced open items directly; the mockup + copy-deck + story-arc are the spec; unify-to-one-name removes the tri-name inconsistency; ground reconciliation surfaced for the plan | This row; `mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/` (README, build-spec, copy-deck, story-arc, whole-site-gallery-v2.html); AskUserQuestion answers 2026-07-08 |

docs/restructure-w0-ledger.md:1:# Restructure W0 — Migration Ledger
docs/PLAIN-ENGLISH.md:76:| 2026-07-08 | **Building — the report got its final look (the owner picked "Ledger", white)** | From the five design candidates, the owner chose **#1 Ledger** — the Swiss-audit-certificate style — with one change: a pure gallery-white background instead of the sample's faintly warm paper. The real `/report` and `/demo` pages were then restyled to that system: clean Helvetica-class type, thin ruled lines, findings numbered 01–16, one ultramarine-blue accent, red/amber severity marks kept strictly separate from the accent, no rounded corners or shadows anywhere. **Not one word of the report changed** — the SIMULATED banner, every finding's plain sentence and four receipts, the tally, and the honesty footer are byte-identical to before; only the clothing changed, and the tests enforce that. One new safety test was added: it fails the build if anyone ever removes the CSS property that keeps the SIMULATED banner printing on its dark ground (without it, a printed copy would silently lose the honesty label as white-on-white). An honest stumble is on the record: during review, the reviewer accidentally reverted the new stylesheet with a careless restore command; the builder restored it byte-exact from its own snapshot, everything re-verified green, and the lesson (back up before any destructive test demonstration) is written down where every future session reads it. Putting the page on a public web address (deploy) remains a separate owner decision. |
docs/reviews/d1-slice-record.md:24:- **Web renderer** — `app/demo/page.tsx` (server metadata) + `components/demo/DemoView.tsx` (Static, no hooks) rendering the COMMITTED transcript golden `expected-demo.json` + `copy.ts` — mirrors the report page exactly (which renders committed golden reports, since the fs-touching engine cannot run in the web build). Two registers per beat (plain lead leads, technical detail under, receipts last). SIMULATED banner. Nav link added. `dmo-*` CSS reusing the report's Ledger system.

docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-06-pub-slice-raw.md:6848:./HANDOFF.md:472:> **▶ Refreshed 2026-06-24 (Claude Code) — UI REDESIGN BUILD: STAGE 1 DONE + COMMITTED + GREEN (`8b8e237`).** The long design thread converged: the owner chose the **"Ledger"** direction (data-as-evidence: `field · value · verdict` rows, premium white + a single vermilion accent), reframed as an **enterprise Merchant-Operations** product (governed/auditable), with a locked content/arc/register spec. **READ FIRST for any continuation: `docs/story-arc-content-spec.md`** (the LOCKED source of truth — the 9-beat insight-led arc; the two-layer rule; the AI-vocabulary rule; honesty) and the design source `mockups/v3-ledger-enterprise.html`. **Stage 1 shipped:** the design-system foundation (`app/globals.css` tokens + self-hosted `next/font` Geist/Geist Mono/JetBrains Mono + the ledger-row primitive) and the **storytelling LANDING as the new front door at `/`** (`app/page.tsx` + `components/landing/*`); the **Overview/Queue moved to `/console`**; `Nav` + `evals/e2e/console.spec.ts` updated (+ a landing smoke test). **REGISTER (verified): professional + operational + layman-legible; professional AI words on the front (AI, AI reviewer, hallucination, fabrication, guardrail); deep ML jargon (entailment, cross-family, eval-locked, κ, RAG, precision/recall) ONLY in opt-in layers — 0 visible leaks.** Honest hero (the overclaim "nothing false ships" was FIXED → "nothing reaches a merchant until every claim is checked against their own data"). **`verify:full` GREEN: typecheck + lint + 192 tests (+2 skipped) + build + 4 e2e.** `lib/` + all logic/data/evals/judge UNTOUCHED.

docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s2-landing-raw.md:489:> **▶▶ DESIGN SAMPLES DELIVERED — FIVE REDESIGN CANDIDATES FOR `/report` AWAIT THE OWNER'S PICK (2026-07-08, seventeenth session — SUPERSEDED by the block above).** On the owner's word (*"give 3 to 5 design samples using all the skills and subagents based the context."*) the design-shotgun ran: five parallel `frontend-specialist` subagents, one visual direction each, all rendering the SAME real committed golden fixture (`expected-report.acp.json` — FAIL · 16 findings · 11 error / 5 warn / 0 info) under the honesty gates (verbatim SIMULATED banner · plain-line-first + four receipts · real tally · footer honesty paragraph · no banned framing). **Durable copies: `mockups/design-samples-2026-07-08/` (5 HTML + README with the verification record).** The five directions: **1 Ledger** (Swiss audit certificate, ultramarine) · **2 Console** (calm forensic terminal, dark blue-grey) · **3 Broadsheet** (serif editorial investigation, oxblood) · **4 Control Room** (audit-dashboard UI, summary rail + severity-striped cards, slate-teal) · **5 Dossier** (evidence case-file, stamp aesthetics — the risk take). **Seat incident (honest record):** all five builders died at their FINAL step on the shared subagent seat limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 9:10am (America/New_York)") AFTER their files landed; the safety classifier was down for their review → the Fable-equivalence review ran INLINE (machine checks all PASS: banner/footer verbatim, 16/16 findings + receipts, tally 11/5/0 recomputed-correct, zero banned framing, zero external URLs, fragment format). Seat later confirmed reset by the owner. **NO product code changed; engine + web views untouched; verify baseline 944+6 stands (docs/mockups-only session).** Recommendation on record (not a decision): #1 Ledger primary · #4 Control Room product-feel alternative · #2 Console personality pick.

This turn the three writer-flagged open items were put to the owner and answered: **① Direction = "Oxblood v2 (burgundy)"** (`whole-site-gallery-v2.html` — premium off-white #FBFBFD ground, ONE burgundy accent #8A2233, grid removed, serif-display+mono, custom monoline SVG icons, scroll-motion, story-arc content); **② Identity = "Unify → Commerce Truth Audit"** (retire the page-title "ActivationOps" + console "Curbside Commons" from the site; the S-11 real-brand pick, e.g. Plumbline, stays a separate open owner act and would rename later); **③ TOTAL_STEPS = 5 confirmed** (landing pipeline copy correct as built). This FIRES the gated product-code implementation (rewrite `app/globals.css` + all pages/components + content, under the full per-slice gate). **Ground reconciliation flagged, not silently resolved:** v2's #FBFBFD vs the already-implemented `/report` `--paper #FFFFFF` (decision-log 2026-07-08 Ledger pick) — to be reconciled at implementation planning (the whole-site rewrite supersedes the interim Ledger `/report` styling, so #FBFBFD becomes the sitewide ground unless the owner says otherwise). Build routes to Opus lane, judged at the Fable-equivalence bar (design-quality standard, this date); deploy stays a SEPARATE owner act (design-first ruling 2026-07-03). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-08) | Owner answered the three surfaced open items directly; the mockup + copy-deck + story-arc are the spec; unify-to-one-name removes the tri-name inconsistency; ground reconciliation surfaced for the plan | This row; `mockups/ultra-modern-2026-07-08/` (README, build-spec, copy-deck, story-arc, whole-site-gallery-v2.html); AskUserQuestion answers 2026-07-08 |
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s2-landing-raw.md:8302:docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-06-pub-slice-raw.md:6848:./HANDOFF.md:472:> **▶ Refreshed 2026-06-24 (Claude Code) — UI REDESIGN BUILD: STAGE 1 DONE + COMMITTED + GREEN (`8b8e237`).** The long design thread converged: the owner chose the **"Ledger"** direction (data-as-evidence: `field · value · verdict` rows, premium white + a single vermilion accent), reframed as an **enterprise Merchant-Operations** product (governed/auditable), with a locked content/arc/register spec. **READ FIRST for any continuation: `docs/story-arc-content-spec.md`** (the LOCKED source of truth — the 9-beat insight-led arc; the two-layer rule; the AI-vocabulary rule; honesty) and the design source `mockups/v3-ledger-enterprise.html`. **Stage 1 shipped:** the design-system foundation (`app/globals.css` tokens + self-hosted `next/font` Geist/Geist Mono/JetBrains Mono + the ledger-row primitive) and the **storytelling LANDING as the new front door at `/`** (`app/page.tsx` + `components/landing/*`); the **Overview/Queue moved to `/console`**; `Nav` + `evals/e2e/console.spec.ts` updated (+ a landing smoke test). **REGISTER (verified): professional + operational + layman-legible; professional AI words on the front (AI, AI reviewer, hallucination, fabrication, guardrail); deep ML jargon (entailment, cross-family, eval-locked, κ, RAG, precision/recall) ONLY in opt-in layers — 0 visible leaks.** Honest hero (the overclaim "nothing false ships" was FIXED → "nothing reaches a merchant until every claim is checked against their own data"). **`verify:full` GREEN: typecheck + lint + 192 tests (+2 skipped) + build + 4 e2e.** `lib/` + all logic/data/evals/judge UNTOUCHED.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:441:The whole-site rewrite **supersedes** the interim Ledger `/report` styling. Sitewide ground = **`#FBFBFD`** (v2 research pick). This changes the already-implemented `/report` + `/demo` from the committed Ledger `--paper #FFFFFF` — that is intended (the whole-site system replaces it). If the owner instead wants the sitewide ground held at `#FFFFFF`, that is a one-token override at planning — flag before S1.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:444:Unlike the Ledger `/report` restyle (which was CONTENT-FROZEN), this redesign **changes content** (story-arc narrative) per the owner's "layout + content" directive. But the **honesty invariants stay byte-frozen**:
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:459:- **S3 — Report + Demo (honesty-critical):** `report/page.tsx` + `ReportView.tsx`, `demo/page.tsx` + `DemoView.tsx` — re-skin to Oxblood, **supersede the Ledger styling**, reconcile ground to `#FBFBFD`; preserve SIMULATED banner byte-verbatim + footer + 16 findings 11/5/0 + the print stylesheet; **consciously update the +3 print-fidelity assertions + report/demo view tests red-green** (they were written for the Ledger system).
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:512:-   ActivationOps — "Ledger Enterprise" design system (Stage 1 foundation)

docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:5270:    16	The whole-site rewrite **supersedes** the interim Ledger `/report` styling. Sitewide ground = **`#FBFBFD`** (v2 research pick). This changes the already-implemented `/report` + `/demo` from the committed Ledger `--paper #FFFFFF` — that is intended (the whole-site system replaces it). If the owner instead wants the sitewide ground held at `#FFFFFF`, that is a one-token override at planning — flag before S1.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:5273:    19	Unlike the Ledger `/report` restyle (which was CONTENT-FROZEN), this redesign **changes content** (story-arc narrative) per the owner's "layout + content" directive. But the **honesty invariants stay byte-frozen**:
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:5288:    34	- **S3 — Report + Demo (honesty-critical):** `report/page.tsx` + `ReportView.tsx`, `demo/page.tsx` + `DemoView.tsx` — re-skin to Oxblood, **supersede the Ledger styling**, reconcile ground to `#FBFBFD`; preserve SIMULATED banner byte-verbatim + footer + 16 findings 11/5/0 + the print stylesheet; **consciously update the +3 print-fidelity assertions + report/demo view tests red-green** (they were written for the Ledger system).
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:5384:   188	  color: #fff;
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-08-s1-shell-raw.md:5479:    12	function ArrowRight({ stroke = "#fff" }: { stroke?: string }) {
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-06-advisory-nits-raw.md:280:- **Part A — web view leg (C4):** `/report` page — `app/report/page.tsx` (server, metadata) → `components/report/ReportView.tsx` (client, surface toggle ACP/UCP) → renders the COMMITTED golden fixture reports (`expected-report.{acp,ucp}.json`, statically imported, bundled). Plain-words line LEADS each finding; the four C2 receipts (claim · reference-row · rule · severity) always visible; header pins specVersion + matchingMode (plain-decoded) + an unmissable SIMULATED banner (C10). Matches the Ledger-Enterprise idiom (rpt-* over the existing `.ds-*` tokens); print stylesheet → clean one-page print, `break-inside: avoid` per finding, honesty labels forced to print. Route prerenders `○ Static`; $0, zero LLM, zero network. Pure transform in `lib/packs/listings/report-view.ts`.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-06-advisory-nits-raw.md:674:> **LABELS — UNCHANGED, all 3 DEFER (run-independent, re-confirmed).** Router ablation `signals_differ:0` again (23 live calls, structurally identical to `strongReflection`); Strategist DEFER by construction, Domain Critic DEFER by R-A3-8 cap, Router DEFER by the structural tie. Ledger "1 earned (Drafter) + 3 deferred". Do NOT re-litigate.

Plain-words line LEADS each finding; the four C2 receipts (claim · reference-row · rule · severity) always visible; header pins specVersion + matchingMode (plain-decoded) + an unmissable SIMULATED banner (C10). Matches the Ledger-Enterprise idiom (rpt-* over the existing `.ds-*` tokens); print stylesheet → clean one-page print, `break-inside: avoid` per finding, honesty labels forced to print. Route prerenders `○ Static`; $0, zero LLM, zero network. Pure transform in `lib/packs/listings/report-view.ts`.

Ledger "1 earned (Drafter) + 3 deferred". Do NOT re-litigate.
